title: AIP Audit Log Entry (v1)
description: One line of the append-only audit log (ADR-0019). The audit log is the foundation of every other guarantee — its hash chain anchors all derived state. Each entry's 'entry_hash' is SHA-256(JCS(entry minus entry_hash)); each entry's 'prev_hash' equals the previous entry's 'entry_hash', except for the bootstrap entry whose prev_hash is 64 zeros.

seq.description: Monotonically increasing 0-based index. The bootstrap entry is seq=0.

prev_hash.description: Hash of the previous entry, or 64 zeros for the bootstrap entry.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC, second precision (microsecond MUST be 0 for canonical reproducibility).

actor.description: Operator-supplied actor identifier (e.g. '@jfhelvetius'). Not authenticated by itself.

action.description: Closed v1 vocabulary. Adding a value is safe; renaming or removing one would invalidate historical chains and requires ADR.

target.description: Canonical URI of the subject of the action. Examples: 'aip:archive/bootstrap', 'aip:evidence/sha256:<64hex>'.

parameters.description: Action-specific parameters as a flat string→string map. Content-derived fields (e.g. size_bytes for ingest_evidence) must agree across archives that ingest the same content (ADR-0044).

schema_version.description: Audit entry schema version. Currently '0.1.0' in the canonical implementation.

entry_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of JCS(entry minus entry_hash). Verifiers recompute and compare.

iso8601UtcSeconds.description: ISO-8601 UTC with second precision and trailing 'Z'. No microseconds, no offset notation.
